entity.summary: Global centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched on July 14, 2017. Binance remained active after its November 2023 coordinated U.S. criminal and civil resolutions, which included a corporate guilty plea, more than USD 4.3 billion in aggregate penalties, leadership change, and enhanced compliance obligations.
entity.notes: The November 2023 U.S. resolution is a major regulatory and criminal-enforcement event but not a shutdown marker. The Binance.com global exchange remained operational; Binance.US is a separate regional platform and is not merged into this entity record.

expected.summary: Centralized exchange launched in 2017 and still operating under the Binance brand.
expected.notes: Official Binance announcement set the exchange launch for 2017-07-14.

changes.summary: Global centralized cryptocurrency exchange launched on July 14, 2017. Binance remained active after its November 2023 coordinated U.S. criminal and civil resolutions, which included a corporate guilty plea, more than USD 4.3 billion in aggregate penalties, leadership change, and enhanced compliance obligations.
changes.notes: The November 2023 U.S. resolution is a major regulatory and criminal-enforcement event but not a shutdown marker. The Binance.com global exchange remained operational; Binance.US is a separate regional platform and is not merged into this entity record.

events.title: Binance entered coordinated U.S. criminal and civil resolutions
events.description: Binance Holdings pleaded guilty to U.S. anti-money-laundering, unlicensed money-transmitting, and sanctions violations and entered coordinated resolutions with DOJ, CFTC, FinCEN, and OFAC requiring more than USD 4.3 billion in aggregate penalties and compliance remediation.
events.notes: The CFTC consent order announced on this date was initially subject to court approval; this event records the coordinated resolution announcement and Binance guilty plea rather than a service shutdown.

evidence.title: Binance and CEO Plead Guilty to Federal Charges in $4B Resolution
evidence.notes: DOJ announcement of the Binance Holdings guilty plea, financial penalty, compliance monitor, and Changpeng Zhao plea and resignation.

evidence.title: Binance and Its CEO Agree to Pay $2.85 Billion for Willfully Evading U.S. Law
evidence.notes: CFTC announcement of the proposed consent order and the coordinated aggregate resolution exceeding USD 4.3 billion.

evidence.title: Binance official exchange platform
evidence.notes: Current official domain confirming continued global exchange operation.
